# Contributing to Spacer

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Spacer! By contributing to this project, you are helping make it better for everyone. Before you get started, please take a moment to read the following guidelines.

## Getting Started

1. Fork the repository and clone it to your local machine.
2. Create a new branch for your contribution: `git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name`.
3. Make your changes and commit them: `git commit -m 'Add your feature'`.
4. Push to the branch: `git push origin feature/your-feature-name`.
5. Open a pull request.

## Contribution Guidelines

### Code of Conduct

Please note that this project is governed by our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you agree to uphold this code.

### Branching Strategy

- `stable`: The stable branch represents the latest stable release.
- `main`: The main branch is used for ongoing development.

### Coding Standards

Follow the coding standards defined in the project. Ensure your code is clean, well-documented, and follows best practices.

### Testing

Make sure your changes pass all existing tests. If you're adding new features, include relevant tests.

## Submitting a Pull Request

1. Provide a clear and descriptive title for your pull request.
2. Clearly explain the purpose of your changes in the pull request description.
3. Reference any related issues or pull requests in your description using the keywords "Closes," "Fixes," or "Resolves."

## Code Reviews

All contributions go through a code review process. Be prepared to address feedback and make necessary changes to your code.

## License

By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the project's [LICENSE](../LICENSE).

---

Thank you for contributing to Spacer! Your involvement is appreciated.
